graded_findings.rationale: This is a real adherence gap because the design document still presents removed log_rate_limit-era controls as current behavior. That stale current-state guidance would mislead future changes to logging admission and configuration.

This is blocking because the trace/context documentation still assigns sampling decisions and counter ownership to the removed LogRateLimiter path. The mismatch affects shared logging semantics rather than incidental prose.

This plan remains materially stale by describing slow PLOG retention as bypassing log_rate_limit instead of the new request and diagnostic sampling model. That is a substantive recovery/observability contract mismatch for the changed logging behavior.

This summary omits the new diagnostic_sample_rate behavior and still documents the removed bypass relationship to log_rate_limit. Because it summarizes implemented behavior, the stale statement is merger-blocking documentation drift.
